briefing.headline: Rig up a Walking Topwater — aggressive pre-spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for pre-spawn with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC hooks get into the fish

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC ho
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In Florida heavy grass, switch to 30-lb braid with a 12-inch monofilament leader (Sufix Elite) for topwater — need to force fish out of hay-field grass or you'll lose them
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, rock, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Palaniuk: 'If you slow down and work topwater in the strike zones, you'll get more bites and the bites you do get hook up a lot better.'
Wheeler: At Santee Cooper, pre-spawn keys were 10 ft and shallower, hard places and rock (hard bottom, rock, gravel), plus brush and stumps. Better if you had both rock and wood but didn't need both

KVD: Clear water on grass lakes = fish can see the bait from farther away (benefit) but can also be spooky (challenge). Fish may be timid — bites are subtle, just bumping the bait. Slow down and keep working the area.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal pre-spawn bass are aggressive and moving shallow. Expect fish in the 4-8ft range on secondary points and channel swings. Falling pressure pulls fish up from staging areas — target the first breakline off spawning flats.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are blowing up but not connecting, pause 3-5 seconds after each strike before resuming
